synthesis_text: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, practice good personal hygiene, including regular hand washing with soap and water [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Wear a face mask in public and when in close contact with others [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Maintain physical or social distancing to reduce transmission risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Keep indoor spaces well-ventilated to minimize airborne transmission [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Avoid sharing personal household items to prevent virus spread [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Stay home if you are feeling unwell or believe you may be infected [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Cover coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Stay up to date on COVID-19 vaccinations to reduce infection risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00100]. Practice physical distancing from individuals showing respiratory symptoms [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00019]. Quarantine for a minimum of 10 days if exposed to COVID-19 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00129].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine, as this helps prevent serious illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Ensuring good indoor airflow can also help prevent the spread of the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Practicing physical distancing reduces the risk of transmission [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Wearing a mask in appropriate settings provides protection for yourself and others [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Maintaining good hygiene by washing hands frequently with soap and water is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Avoiding close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms is also advisable [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Additionally, covering your coughs and sneezes helps prevent spreading the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure is crucial for early detection [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Finally, staying informed about the latest COVID-19 guidance from local authorities can help you make informed decisions [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00022].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, individuals should avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing, keeping at least 1 metre distance from others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Wearing a mask in public places where community transmission is present and physical distancing is not possible is also recommended [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Frequent handwashing with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub is crucial [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Keeping indoor spaces well ventilated helps reduce the risk of virus transmission [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Staying informed about the latest COVID-19 guidance from local authorities is important [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00029]. Getting vaccinated can help reduce the spread of the virus and its mutations [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]. Regularly cleaning and disinfecting frequently touched surfaces is also advised [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00026]. Finally, testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure to the virus is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].
